tasks.description: Daily 2am PT poker intel briefing for The Only Friends Podcast, posted to Discord #intel-staging
Daily 3am PT world news & trends briefing for The Only Friends Podcast, posted to Discord #intel-staging
Daily 4am PT memes & culture drop for The Only Friends Podcast, posted to Discord #intel-staging
Daily 7am PT run-of-show for The Only Friends Podcast. Posts a condensed single-page brief to #run-of-show and the full detailed ROS to #intel-staging.

Daily 8am PT thumbnail generator for The Only Friends Podcast — reads ROS from #run-of-show, produces A/B for biggest story + alternative for 2nd biggest, judge enforces ≥70 with rebuilds, posts 3 images to #thumbnails.

Daily 8:15am PT producer audit before 8:30 pre-production for The Only Friends Podcast. Audits #intel-staging / ROS / thumbnails, promotes assets + design requests out of #intel-staging, and acts on Matt's manual notes in #producer.
Primary 7:30am PT show-graphics sweep for The Only Friends Podcast — reads #graphic-design requests, generates tone-driven 1280×720 graphics via Nano Banana Pro, posts to #assets
Late 8:05am PT show-graphics sweep for The Only Friends Podcast — catches late adds and any graphics flagged by thumbnails, finishes before 8:15 producer audit
Daily 5am PT viral-hand research cron for The Only Friends Podcast. Summary of ranked hands → #intel-staging; clip link → #assets. Feeds the B block.
Writes /Users/mjb11/Documents/Claude/Projects/Media Company Infrastructure/web/data.json every 5 minutes AND pushes the change to GitHub so the Mission Control Pages site serves fresh data.
tasks.schedule: Every 5 minutes, every hour, every day

1489392874029187304.content: berkey11: spawn the designer with 4 Nano Banana comps of the OF badge — we're going for a sophisticated look. Can subtly include generic imagery like suits and a mic, just do it abstractly. Lock in the color palette, just be sure to include more than just blue, white or negative space is fine.

**Graphic Designer:**

Now converting the JPGs to PNGs and dropping them in the outbox.All four PNGs are in the outbox. Here's what was built:

---

**OF Badge — 4 Concept Comps**
*(All generated via Nano Banana Pro / gemini-3-pro-image-preview)*

**Comp A — Charcoal Gold**
Deep charcoal black base, "OF" serif monogram in champagne gold, burnished bronze hairline circle border with subtle herringbone textile texture suggesting tailored fabric. Warmest, most classic of the four — luxury whiskey label energy.

**Comp B — Burgundy Navy**
Oxblood burgundy meets midnight navy split-field within the badge circle. Abstract suit geometry in negative space at center, waveform dot arc for the mic suggestion, ivory and rose-gold accents. Most dramatic tonal contrast — poker room at 2am.

**Comp C — Cream Forest Brass**
Inverted palette — warm cream/off-white field, forest green and aged brass as accent tones. The most editorial and Swiss-influenced. Unique in the set; would stand out on a light-background feed without losing sophistication.

**Comp D — Plum Platinum**
Near-black plum base with violet undertones, concentric platinum rings suggesting a mic grille from above, pinstripe radiating texture at low opacity, warm amber + steel-blue tonal contrast. Most cinematic/nocturnal — high-end spirits label or luxury wax seal feel.

Let me know which direction resonates (or if you want to mash-up elements from multiple comps) and I'll push it to vector.
berkey11: ok, rerun it but stay true to our blue color scheme. Additionally be a little less abstract as these just look like random geometric figures

**Graphic Designer:**

All four generated. Now converting to PNGs and dropping them in the outbox.All 4 PNGs are in the outbox. Here's what was built — all generated via Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image-preview):

---

**Comp A — Navy + Champagne Gold**
Deep navy `#003366` base. Suit jacket silhouette centered, lapels and sleeves clearly readable. Bold serif "OF" monogram overlaid in champagne gold. Gold circular border ring, subtle embossed texture. The most classic/heraldic of the four — luxury members club energy.

**Comp B — Royal Blue + Chrome Silver**
Vivid royal blue with a clearly recognizable upright condenser microphone at center — grille detail, stand, the works. "OF" in bright white with silver shadow above the mic. Sound wave arcs in silver flanking. Double-ring chrome border. Audio brand meets premium broadcast aesthetic.

**Comp C — Midnight Blue + Warm Gold Filigree**
The most detailed comp — suited figure and condenser mic together in gold line-art, "OF" arced at the top, "ONLY FRIENDS" in small gold spaced caps along the bottom arc. Ornate gold filigree border. Deep midnight `#001845`. Heraldic, almost like a wax seal or whiskey label.

**Comp D — Cerulean/Steel Gradient + Platinum Silver**
Most contemporary of the four. Large bold "OF" in white metallic takes foreground, suit-and-tie silhouette behind in subtle darker blue, small mic icon below. Polished steel silver beveled border. Cerulean-to-steel gradient gives it depth without going dark. Premium podcast/media brand feel.

Let me know which direction(s) to push to vector in Illustrator — I can mash up elements from multiple comps or dial in a specific one.
berkey11: I don't really like the suit concept. I do like the idea of a subtle image in the background though.

Got it. Before I rerun these, what background imagery direction appeals to you? A few options:

- **Poker table / cards** — subtle card edge or felt texture visible behind the badge
- **Stage / spotlight** — lighting rig or stage floor implied
- **Mic + stand only** — ditch the suit entirely, keep just the microphone as the readable foreground element
- **Texture / pattern** — herringbone weave, pinstripe, or geometric pattern that suggests luxury/sophistication without depicting anything literal
- **Crowd silhouette** — blurred figures in background suggesting an audience

Or something else entirely? Just want to nail the vibe before I regenerate.
berkey11: Yeah maybe we go crowd silhouette and then work in abstract card suit(s) and/or mic

**Graphic Designer:**

All 4 PNGs are in the outbox. Here's the breakdown — all generated via Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image-preview):

---

**Comp A — Navy + Champagne Gold, Suit Symbols**
Deep navy badge, bold serif "OF" in champagne gold, abstract spade/diamond accents at cardinal positions, blurred poker room crowd barely visible in the background. Most classic/heraldic of the four.

**Comp B — Royal Blue + Silver, Mic Forward**
Royal-to-steel blue gradient, "OF" in silver-white metallic, abstract condenser mic in silver line art beneath the monogram, heart/club suit symbols at the badge edges. Most broadcast/audio-forward.

**Comp C — Midnight Blue + Gold Filigree, Full Suite**
Deepest, most ornate — all four suit symbols in gold around the "OF", "ONLY FRIENDS" arced along the bottom, minimal mic icon below the monogram, filigree border ring. The most detailed and wax-seal/whiskey-label in feel.

**Comp D — Cerulean/Navy Gradient + Platinum, Suits + Mic**
Most contemporary — cerulean-to-navy radial gradient, platinum "OF", spade/diamond in fine platinum line work, slim mic silhouette at base. Clean hierarchy, premium without being heavy.

Let Matt know which direction (or element mix) to push to Illustrator and I'll build the vector.
berkey11: Call the graphic designer and feed him these specific adjustments

**Graphic Designer:**

Now converting to PNG and dropping in the outbox:All 4 PNGs delivered to the outbox. Here's the breakdown — all via Nano Banana Pro (gemini-3-pro-image-preview):

---

**Comp A — Navy + Poker Felt**
Deep navy base. Gold condenser mic as the clear hero. Behind it: dark green/blue poker felt texture at low opacity — just enough to nod to the poker world without screaming it. Champagne gold "OF" serif + bottom arc lettering. Classic members club energy.

**Comp B — Midnight Blue + Stage Spotlight**
Midnight-to-royal blue gradient. Chrome/silver vintage broadcast mic centered. Subtle warm amber spotlight cone hitting a stage floor behind it — cinematic, live-show atmosphere. "OF" in white + gold arc. The most cinematic of the four.

**Comp C — Cerulean + Herringbone Texture**
Cerulean blue base. Modern gold condenser mic, prominent. Background: ultra-subtle herringbone weave pattern in slightly lighter blue — luxury fabric feel, zero literalism. Platinum ring border. The most editorial/refined — best for merch, apparel tags, print applications.

**Comp D — Midnight Blue + Crowd Silhouette**
Near-black midnight base. Gold mic in the foreground. Behind it: softly blurred audience silhouettes — suggests a live poker show or broadcast event, deeply atmospheric. Ornate gold ring border. The most dramatic/cinematic of the set.

---

**Designer's pick:** Comp B or D for digital/social (most immediate visual impact), Comp C for any physical/merch application (texture reads beautifully in print). Let me know which direction to push to Illustrator for the final vector build.

ros.content:
📋 **Run of Show — Wednesday, April 22, 2026 — EP. —**

**A BLOCK:** Matusow Says High-Roller Earnings Are a Mirage — Chidwick Admits $5–10M on $76M Lifetime
**B BLOCK:** WSOP Rings Debut in Texas Tomorrow (While The Lodge Sits Frozen) | USPO Championship Tonight
**C BLOCK:** Zobian's USPO #9 + Children's Nonprofit / Hellmuth Home Game Ep 20 "Quads" Moment / Wemby Concussion Tanks Spurs Futures / Cartillier's Cinderella WSOPC Win

📺 **Top YT title:** "Stephen Chidwick Just Admitted He's Only Kept $10M of His $76M in Lifetime Poker Cashes"
🎬 **Best clip:** A Block ~0:14:00–0:17:00 — Matusow's "mirage" quote + Chidwick's $5–10M / $76M reply + Matt's "what did you keep" line

*[cowork] Run-of-Show Agent · April 22, 2026 · 7:25 AM PT*

---

**🎯 COLD OPEN (~2 min)**

Lead with the number, then the math, then the question.

**Suggested line:** *"Mike Matusow went on record Monday and said the quiet part out loud: every number you see on Hendon Mob is a mirage. These guys aren't winning what you think they're winning. And then — because this community actually does its policing in public — Stephen Chidwick replied. On record. With the real number. Seventy-six million dollars in career cashes. Five to ten million in actual profit. That's every Triton main event, every Super High Roller Bowl, every Aria $25K for a decade of his life — and he's kept somewhere between seven and thirteen cents on the dollar. That's your A block. Because if Matusow is wrong and Chidwick is the ceiling, the whole thing is even worse than he said."*

**Alternate (number-forward):** *"Stephen Chidwick has $76 million in career tournament cashes. How much do you think he actually kept? Five to ten. That's the answer he gave yesterday. Welcome to Wednesday."*

*Prep: Conrad — cue Matusow tweet + Chidwick reply side-by-side. Andy — pull Chidwick Hendon Mob page for full-screen.*

**🔴 A BLOCK (~40 min) — Matusow Calls High-Roller Earnings a Mirage. Then the Pros Told Us What They Actually Keep.**

**Why this A block:** Matusow Monday — the Hendon Mob numbers are a lie. Then — for the first time on public record — high-rolling pros broke the ledger. Davies put the "normal" number at ~50% own action. Chidwick put HIS number at $5–10M on $76M. Deeb dropped the Peters "money problems aren't poker losses" hint. The one question every rec asks — *"do these guys actually win that money?"* — got answered by the people with the ledger. Maximum thumbnail. No repeat of any ROS in the last 3 shows. Does NOT repeat Apr 20's Schulman K-4.

**Setup (7 min):**
- Tuesday Apr 21: **Dylan Linde** posts publicly that **David Peters** owes him ~$25K of a $50K Triton staking loss, has only paid half. Peters denies.
- **Matusow** enters: *"high-roller tournament earnings are fake — they're playing with backer money, the Hendon Mob numbers are a mirage."*
- Replies within 24 hours:
  • **Seth Davies:** high-stakes regs *"typically"* keep ~50% of their own action
  • **Stephen Chidwick:** publicly puts his number at **$5–10M profit on $76M career cashes**
  • **Shaun Deeb:** Peters' problems *"aren't poker losses"* — points elsewhere, doesn't name
  • **Josh Arieh:** defended MTT pros as a class

**A BLOCK — Talking Points**

1. **Matusow is right, but the real number is worse.** Chidwick is probably the 95th-percentile profit case and he's at ~10%. What does the median high-roller actually net?
2. **The Hendon Mob lie.** Every media outlet quotes lifetime cashes as if they were earnings. We helped build that lie. What would honest reporting look like?
3. **Davies' 50% number.** Is "typically 50% own action" really the ceiling, or is Seth flexing? Matt's floor estimate. Jesse's floor estimate.
4. **What Deeb is hinting at.** *"Peters' money problems aren't poker losses."* Read between the lines — sports, crypto, something else? Don't guess; frame the question.
5. **Does this kill high-roller content or grow it?** Transparency could pop the viewer-pro gap and 2x the audience. Veldhuis / Negreanu stream-math comp.

**A BLOCK — Counterpoint + Tangents + Seed Line**

**Counterpoint/steelman (~22 min):** *"Matusow is doing what Matusow does — torching the class while filming CBS specials and grinding Hellmuth's $25/$50 home game on camera the same week. It's bit content, not economics."* Let Matt dismantle: Matusow is correct because **Chidwick — who has nothing to gain — confirmed the math.** The specific dollar amount is the thing. You can dismiss Matusow. You can't dismiss Chidwick's number.

**Natural tangent at ~25 min:** Matusow was on Hellmuth's Home Game Ep 20 Monday — filming a cash game with Phil while also lighting up the econ debate on X. Cross-ref the "when you're the greatest you make quads" clip (C block Item 2) with the "you're all lying about your wins" quote — same 48 hours. Who's the bigger poker character right now, Phil or Mike? Matt's seed: *"Both. Same character, different angles."*

**Natural tangent at ~32 min:** Linde vs. Peters direct — is Peters bust, is this a backer dispute, or is this a grown-man publicly called out on a $25K tab? Staking norms: if you lose, you pay. If you can't pay, you communicate. Silence is the crime, not the amount.

**Matt's seed line:** *"Mike Matusow said it ugly and Matusow is going to pay for saying it ugly. But Stephen Chidwick just put the real number on the board and we all heard him. So from here on out — when somebody says they won eight million dollars on the PGT, the honest follow-up is: okay, what did you keep?"*

**🟠 B BLOCK (~30 min) — WSOP Rings Cross the Texas Border (While The Lodge Is Frozen) + USPO Finale Tonight**

**Segment 1 (~18 min): First-Ever WSOP Circuit in Texas Opens Tomorrow — at TCH Austin, Not The Lodge**

@tjreidpoker posted the full 12-day schedule for the first-ever WSOP Circuit in the state of Texas. **Thursday Apr 23 – May 4** at **Texas Card House Austin.**
• **$600 Mini Main, $500K GTD (Apr 23–27)**
• **$400 Ladies (Apr 26)**
• **$400 Monster (Apr 27–30)**
• Full mixed slate: Big O, 8-Game, HORSE, 2-7 SD, PLO
• Jeff Platt confirms he's on the ground

**Why this matters:** The Lodge — Texas' biggest room, Doug Polk's flagship — has been **shuttered under civil forfeiture** since the TABC raid. While The Lodge sits frozen, the WSOP is handing out **gold rings at a competitor** down the road. Selective enforcement isn't a theory anymore; it's a series calendar.

**Talking Points:**
1. **The optics are catastrophic for Texas AG.** $2M frozen at The Lodge → WSOP blesses a TCH series two weeks later. How is this "legal ambiguity" and not "picking winners"?
2. **Does the ring legitimize TCH as the new Texas flagship, or accelerate the Lodge's civil case?** If you're Polk, what's your play?
3. **What the series tells us about WSOP's Texas read.** Caesars legal wouldn't greenlight rings in Texas if they thought gold was at risk. They did. So what do they know?
4. **$600 Mini Main / $500K GTD — new model for legal-gray Texas poker?** Small buy-ins, big guarantees, rings for credibility.
5. **Matt's prompt:** *"If you're a TCH reg who's been playing $2/$5 all year — do you enter the $600 knowing the attention is radioactive right now?"*

*Source: x.com/tjreidpoker | pgt.com/us-poker-open*

**B BLOCK — Segment 2: USPO Championship Event #10 Tonight**

**Segment 2 (~10 min): $25.6K Championship Runs Today — Wilson Is Still the Story**

The 2026 U.S. Poker Open wraps tonight at PokerGO Studio with the **$25,600 Championship (Event #10).** Through 9 events the series has produced **seven different winners** — Wilson (x2), Deng, Andrews, Kristen Foxen, Simao, Alex Foxen, Zobian. **Brock Wilson enters as USPO points leader.** If he wins again, that's **+6 PGT titles in 2026** — in four months.

**Talking Points:**
1. **Wilson PGT pace check.** Six titles by end of April would put him on a historic trajectory. Andy: compare to Soverel's record-setting year pace.
2. **"Everyone wins once" narrative.** 9 events, 7 winners — is USPO deeper than ever, or is the field softer than Triton?
3. **Is the $25.6K Championship the cleanest measure of who's hot?** Or do you need Triton/PGT Main for that?
4. **Matt's prompt:** *"Who wins tonight — Wilson, Foxen, or a field guy?"*

*Source: pgt.com/us-poker-open*

⚠️ **Hand of the Day:** No qualifying hand in #hand-analysis today — **5th consecutive no-find.** Sweep note from George: Triton X + PokerNews + r/poker + r/LivePokerTournaments all checked, nothing over threshold. Skip the dedicated hand segment; B block is the industry deep-dive instead. **Flag for Matt:** the hand-sweep tool has been flat for a week — worth a protocol review if this continues.

**😂 C BLOCK (~15 min) — Light, Clip-Ready, + Wemby for the Degens**

**Item 1 (~4 min): Aram Zobian Wins USPO Event #9 ($292,800) — And Runs a Children's Nonprofit**
Armenian-American pro **Aram Zobian** took down USPO Event #9 ($15K NLH) Monday for **$292,800.** Heads-up: his K♠6♠ out-flopped Brandon Wilson's A♥9♥ on a **paired-sixes board.** 61 entries, $915K pool. Stacked FT: **Chino Rheem 3rd, Marius Gierse 4th, Nicholas Seward 5th.**
**Hook:** Zobian co-founded the **Natna Children's Foundation** — feeding and educating war-affected kids in Ethiopia's Tigray region. Clean palate cleanser after the Matusow drama.
**Angle:** *"You want a reminder that poker players aren't all characters? Zobian just ran K-6 into A-9 for $293K — and he's sending a slice of it to Tigray."*
*Source: pokernews.com/news/2026/04/aram-zobian-wins-uspo-event-9-51083.htm*

**Item 2 (~4 min): Hellmuth's Home Game Ep 20 — "When You're the Greatest, You Make Quads"**
CBS Sports dropped Ep 20 Monday. $25/$50 cash at Grand Sierra Reno. Lineup: **Phil, his son Phillip "P3" Hellmuth III, and Mike Matusow** (same Matusow nuking the econ of the sport on X that day). Headline: Hellmuth makes quads and delivers *"When you're the greatest, you make quads."* **P3 books +$27,925**; **Randy "3Coin" Sadler felted for –$39K.**
**Angle:** Perfect cross-ref for A block — Matusow the character on CBS vs. Matusow the econ critic on X in the same 24 hours.
*Source: pokernews.com/news/2026/04/hellmuths-home-game-episode-20-recap-51082.htm*

**C BLOCK continued — Items 3 & 4**

**Item 3 (~3 min): Wemby Face-Plants in Game 2 — Biggest Sportsbook Event of the Bracket**
Victor Wembanyama fell **face-first** to the court in Q2 last night, concussion protocol, no timeline. Spurs lost **106–103** to Portland, series tied 1-1. **Scoot dropped 31.** Wemby was the +EV play in every Spurs series — props market cratered overnight.
**Angle:** *"Sports-betting degen corner — if you had Spurs futures, you got chopped. Where do you move tonight?"* (Andy prompt: Suns-Thunder Game 2 tonight — Polymarket has Thunder at 92%, OKC is the 51% title favorite.)
*Source: espn.com/nba | polymarket.com/markets/sports*

**Item 4 (~2 min, CUT FIRST): Matthieu Cartillier Wins WSOPC Aix-en-Provence for €150K — On His 3rd Career Cash**
Fresh today. **783 entries, €1.01M pool.** Cartillier beat Michel Leibgorin HU. Hendon Mob: only his **3rd lifetime cash** — and it's a gold ring.
**Angle:** 30-second Cinderella beat — *"poker's a game anyone can win once."*
*Source: pokernews.com/news/2026/04/poker-player-s-third-tournament-cash-is-for-a-wsopc-main-eve-51087.htm*

⚠️ **No memes/culture drop today** — culture-research bridge failed (same Chrome-session issue as Apr 20 run). If Matt wants a viral clip, pull from the Wemby fall tape. Alt plan: cut Item 4 and extend Hellmuth quads moment to 6 min.

**🔚 CLOSE (~3 min)**

**Tease tomorrow:** USPO Championship result + WSOPC Texas opening night — we'll break the field, the controversy, and whether The Lodge files anything publicly in response. Also: **NFL Draft** opens in Pittsburgh tomorrow night (auto-include for Matt), and **Tesla earnings** tonight will set the Thursday market tone.

**CTA:** *"Subscribe on YouTube, follow on X — live Mon–Fri, 10 to noon Pacific. If you clicked in for the Chidwick number, the rest of this week is more where that came from. We're here every day."*

**Sign-off suggestion:** *"Hendon Mob says seventy-six million. Chidwick says ten. The math is the story. Back tomorrow."*
